Professor Burr said th1t It was
highly satisfactory to note that his
recommendation Of two years ago
that the city install : well and tap
the underground supply near the
upriver pumping station had been
carried into effect and resulted In
giving the city q practically mex
haustible supply Of the purest
water. He congratulated the city
on having this great boon, and said
that IL solves the question Of a
water supply for years.


Professor Burr recommended
that another well be installed as a
precautionary measure, the new
well to be located within about 500
feet of the first one. He COM
mented on the present overtaxed
condition of the pumping plant and
Endorsed the plan Of 1000, 900 gal
Ions additional daily pumpage for
the high pressure system for the
higher elevations. With this added
pumping capacity a portion Of the
pumps can be shut down at times
for repairs or rotation, whereas
this is impossible at the present.


The plan for an auxiliary electric
plant to furnish power m case Of a
break In the water power or In the
event of low water was Endorsed.
